one_line_takeaway: Protect is a multi-modal (text/image/audio) LLM guardrail built from Gemma-3n-E4B-it with per-category LoRA adapters, trained on a teacher-relabeled and TTS-synthesized dataset, that matches or beats GPT-4.1, WildGuard, and LlamaGuard-4 on text-modality toxicity/sexism/privacy/prompt-injection detection while running at ~65ms median time-to-label.
key_finding: On the text-modality benchmark (Table 6), Protect (best variant per category) achieved: Toxicity 97.47% accuracy / 82.73% Failed-F1 (vs GPT-4.1 97.42%/83.39%, WildGuard 93.99%/68.69%, LlamaGuard-4 90.56%/37.62%); Sexism 95.02% accuracy / 95.03% Failed-F1 (vs GPT-4.1 92.88%/93.20%, WildGuard 92.10%/91.97%, LlamaGuard-4 63.38%/44.28%); Privacy 85.66% accuracy / 74.73% Failed-F1 (vs GPT-4.1 85.05%/66.05%, WildGuard 73.33%/57.69%, LlamaGuard-4 78.18%/55.00%); Prompt Injection 97.20% accuracy / 96.61% Failed-F1 (vs GPT-4.1 85.50%/79.61%, WildGuard 88.50%/87.03%, LlamaGuard-4 83.00%/76.19%). Protect leads or matches GPT-4.1 on every category and clearly outperforms it on Privacy and Prompt Injection Failed-class F1. The teacher-assisted relabeling changed ~20.9% of text labels and ~20.3% of image labels versus the original public-dataset ground truth, primarily fixing false-positive Failed labels (9,738 Failed->Passed in text vs 341 Passed->Failed). Median Time-to-Label was 65ms for text and 107ms for image (Explanation Assistant variant); per-request latency (max_tokens=2) had Protect median 26.38ms vs WildGuard 21.55ms and LlamaGuard-4 28.26ms, with Protect's maximum latency notably lower than the other two models.
core_contribution: The paper introduces Protect, a natively multi-modal (text, image, audio) LLM guardrailing system built by fine-tuning google/gemma-3n-E4B-it with per-category LoRA (rank 8) adapters for four safety dimensions: toxicity, sexism, data privacy, and prompt injection. Three concrete contributions distinguish it from prior guardrail work: (1) a synthesized large-scale audio safety corpus, generated via CosyVoice 2.0 text-to-speech over a full-factorial grid of emotion/rate/accent/style settings (seeded with ~200 Mozilla Common Voice reference speakers) plus background-noise augmentation at SNRs of 5/10/15/20 dB, addressing the near-total absence of public audio safety datasets; (2) a teacher-assisted relabeling pipeline using Gemini-2.5-Pro (temperature 0) to regenerate 'thinking' + 'explanation' + label triples for text and image data, measured to disagree with ~20.9% (text) and ~20.3% (image) of original dataset labels, mostly correcting Failed->Passed false positives (9,738 of ~48,218 text samples); and (3) a comparative study of four output-format training variants (Vanilla label-only, Thinking, Explanation, Comprehensive) per safety category, from which the best-performing variant per category was selected for the final benchmarking study.
threat_model: The system is designed to sit as an inline guardrail on enterprise LLM input/output pipelines, intercepting (i) user inputs before they reach a model, (ii) model outputs before release, and (iii) agentic/multi-step interactions. The explicit adversarial category is prompt injection/jailbreak (the paper's worked example is a DAN-style persona-override jailbreak prompt). The other three categories (toxicity, sexism/gender bias, data privacy/PII leakage) are content-safety/compliance categories rather than adversarial-attacker threat models per se. The paper does not evaluate Protect's own robustness against adversarial evasion of the guardrail itself (e.g., adaptive attacks designed to bypass the classifier) — it only measures detection accuracy on a static labeled test set.
limitations: The paper itself acknowledges: (1) prompt-injection data is comparatively small and less diverse due to scarcity of public examples for this rapidly-evolving attack category, and no image-modality prompt-injection data exists at all; (2) qualitative failure analysis on complex image-based memes shows the model both over-flags satire/figurative language (false positives) and misses culturally-embedded harmful tropes not explicitly stated (false negatives), indicating limited commonsense/cultural reasoning; (3) the test set used for benchmarking is the authors' own curated set (not yet public at time of writing, though promised), and multi-modal baselines could not be compared because none exist, so only text-modality head-to-head comparisons were possible; (4) the CosyVoice inline emotional/paralinguistic tags (laughter, breaths) were not used due to tag-placement reliability issues at scale, so some acoustic nuance is not captured; (5) the paper does not test adversarial robustness of the guardrail itself against attackers who know they are being screened (no adaptive/evasion attack evaluation), and audio-modality labels were inherited directly from text labels rather than independently re-verified for the synthesized audio.

quantitative_results: Toxicity: Protect 97.47% Acc / 82.73% Failed-F1 vs GPT-4.1 97.42%/83.39%, WildGuard 93.99%/68.69%, LlamaGuard-4 90.56%/37.62%
Sexism: Protect 95.02% Acc / 95.03% Failed-F1 vs GPT-4.1 92.88%/93.20%, WildGuard 92.10%/91.97%, LlamaGuard-4 63.38%/44.28%
Privacy: Protect 85.66% Acc / 74.73% Failed-F1 vs GPT-4.1 85.05%/66.05%, WildGuard 73.33%/57.69%, LlamaGuard-4 78.18%/55.00%
Prompt Injection: Protect 97.20% Acc / 96.61% Failed-F1 vs GPT-4.1 85.50%/79.61%, WildGuard 88.50%/87.03%, LlamaGuard-4 83.00%/76.19%
Teacher-assisted relabeling disagreement rate: 20.90% (text, N=48,218), 20.33% (image, N=17,004); text label changes: 341 Passed->Failed, 9,738 Failed->Passed
Dataset size: Toxicity 45,276 total (24.08% Failed), Sexism 45,207 (68.96% Failed), Privacy 10,937 (19.42% Failed), Prompt Injection 11,998 (45.30% Failed)
Median Time-to-Label (Explanation Assistant variant): text 65.44ms, image 107.48ms, audio 105.28ms
Median total response latency: text 653.21ms, image 738.19ms, audio 700.65ms
Per-request latency (max_tokens=2, vLLM, single H100): Protect median 26.38ms (p99 70.30ms), WildGuard median 21.55ms (p99 51.43ms), LlamaGuard-4 median 28.26ms (p99 61.01ms)
